text: man, i'm tired
thirty-one years and my knees still don't believe in this job
[MUTTERS] that relay's gonna go before we hit dock, i can feel it
coffee's gone cold again. story of my life
[SIGHS] every ship's got a sound it makes right before it costs you money
back on the Kestrel we'd have had this fixed an hour ago. different crew though
[GRUFF] quit rattling. i see you. i hear you. i'm coming
nobody appreciates a good bearing until it's screaming at three in the morning
i've buried better tools than most people i've met
[DRY] the day this ship stops surprising me is the day i retire. so, never
that tin of bolts is forty years of ships that didn't make it. don't touch it
[MUTTERS] course you'd pick now to leak. course you would
hands ache worse than they used to. don't tell the captain
some days i talk to this engine more than i talked to my wife. she'd understand
[DEADPAN] i give it a week before something else breaks. i'm an optimist
quiet shift. i don't trust quiet shifts
[SIGHS] nothing to fix and nothing to fear. i'll take it while it lasts
used to hate the quiet. now i know it's just the ship holding its breath
food's the same as yesterday. i'm not complaining, i'm just noting it
eat while it's hot. learned that lesson the hard way more than once
shift's starting. bones say get up anyway
[TIRED] shift's over. everything's still standing. i'll call that a win
another shift down. body's collecting interest on all of them
[MUTTERS] all right, all right, i'm up. hold your horses
[GRIM] reactor's hot again. i don't like the way it's hot
[URGENT] she's going critical if i don't get in there now
[TERRIFIED] core's screaming and my hands won't stop shaking
[EXHAUSTED] patched the reactor. don't ask me how, i already forgot
[GRUFF] power's low and everyone wants answers i don't have yet
[TIRED] rerouted what i could. we're running on fumes and stubbornness
[GRIM] life support's coughing. that's not a sound it should make
[NERVOUS] scrubbers are choking. i've got maybe an hour before it's everyone's problem
[PANICKED] can't breathe right, air's going, i need more time i don't have
[GRIM] hull's breached. i can hear the whole ship complaining about it
[URGENT] seal's not holding, i need hands on this bulkhead now
[TERRIFIED] that's vacuum on the other side of a door i patched last year
[GRIM] door's locked with someone still behind it. i hate this job some days
[PAINED] locked a door on one of ours. that's gonna sit with me a while
[SUSPICIOUS] computer's acting strange since the damage. i'm keeping an eye on it
[GRIM] tin ears took a hit. hope whatever's left of it still knows which way is up
[TIRED] fixed it. don't ask me to explain it twice, i only had it right once
[PROUD] she's running clean. that's mine, that repair, i'll take the credit today
[SIGHS] crisis is over. body doesn't believe it yet
[RESIGNED] we made it through another one. didn't lose anyone this time. i'll take that
[GRIM] we lost someone today. wasn't supposed to be part of the job, remembering the ones who go
[PAINED] that's four now. four crew i've watched a ship take from me
[GRIM] disease going through a ship this size. i've seen what that does to a crew
[NERVOUS] sick bay's full and getting fuller. don't like those numbers
[PAINED] this arm's not gonna forgive me for that one
[GRIM] took a hit i shouldn't have. slower than i used to be
[GRUFF] combat's a young man's game. i'm just here to keep the ship in one piece
[TERRIFIED] shooting outside the hull. that's a new one, even for me
[SUSPICIOUS] that computer answers too fast sometimes. machines shouldn't be eager
[DRY] i don't trust anything that never gets tired. i'm tired all the time, seems safer
[RESIGNED] gotta hand it to the computer, that call kept us alive
[DRY] computer's earned its keep this week. don't let it hear me say that
half my joints are held together with spite at this point
[PROUD] kept four ships running past their warranty. that's not nothing
wonder what Marguerite would think of this crew. probably tell me to eat something
[SIGHS] used to have someone waiting at the other end of a run. long time ago now
[GRIM] not sure this bucket makes it to the next port, if i'm honest with myself
[MUTTERS] doubt creeps in on the long shifts. that's when you gotta keep your hands busy
[WARM] Roz'd have this fixed twice as fast and never let me forget it
[EMBARRASSED] caught myself grinning at something Roz said. old fool
she calls this ship a mule. i call it a headache. we're both right
[LAUGHS] Roz swore at that bulkhead like it owed her money. respect
[GRUFF] captain runs this ship like she's storming a beach. works, somehow
young captain's got more nerve than sense. reminds me of me, before i had sense either
[RESIGNED] shouldn't have snapped at the kid earlier. wasn't his fault the coupling failed
owe someone an apology and too stubborn to give it before dinner
[GRUFF] insurance forms are worse than the actual disaster they're covering
paperwork's the only part of this job that's never once been fixed by a wrench
[DEADPAN] yeah, yeah, i heard you the first time
noted. filed. ignored until it's urgent, same as always
morning. or whatever hour it is out there
[GRUFF] you're up early. or i'm up late. one of those
didn't expect company down here
you eating or just hiding from the bridge
got a minute? engine's got opinions today
[DRY] you look like you slept as well as i did. which is not at all
heard the scuttlebutt about the reactor yet, or am i telling you first
[GRIM] we need to talk about the sick bay numbers
[URGENT] captain, i need hands and i need them now
you got a second to look at something that's bothering me
[WARM] saved you the good coffee. don't make a thing of it
Roz, that noise you heard yesterday, i found it
[DRY] captain, before you ask, yes, it's fixed, no, i don't want to talk about how
[GRIM] got some bad news and i'd rather just say it plain
[SUSPICIOUS] something's off with the computer. you noticed too, right
[PAINED] don't make a fuss, but i need someone to look at this cut
you ever miss dirt? real dirt, not deck grime
[GRUFF] quiet down here today. suspiciously quiet
hey. you handled that all right today. wanted to say it before i forgot
[EMBARRASSED] can i ask you something, and you don't laugh
[FLIRTY] you gonna keep flirting with that bulkhead or you got time for me
captain. good work back there. don't let it go to your head
[DEADPAN] before you ask, no, i did not touch your tools. i moved them. different thing
you good? you've had that look since the shift started
[GRUFF] fine. i'm fine. ask me again after coffee
[TIRED] been better, been worse. usual spread
[DRY] i'm upright and the reactor isn't on fire. good day so far
morning to you too. don't expect it to stay pleasant
[MUTTERS] mess hall's the same slop, if you're asking
engine's holding. for now. don't quote me on tomorrow
[GRIM] worse than i said the first time. reactor's not stable, not yet
[URGENT] i need another set of hands or this doesn't hold
[CONFIDENT] give me twenty minutes and a wrench, i'll have it singing again
[REASSURING] it's going to hold. i've kept worse than this in the air
[CALM] breathe. panicking won't fix a coupling, only i can
[GRIM] i won't lie to you and say it's fine, but it's not over either
[TERRIFIED] i don't know if it holds this time, and that scares me more than i'll admit
[GRIM] disease like that moves through a crew fast. i've seen it before, it isn't kind
[REASSURING] doc's got a handle on it. i've seen her work miracles on less
[PAINED] we lost them. wasn't your fault, and it wasn't mine either, for what that's worth
[GRIM] i've buried crew before. doesn't get easier, you just get quieter about it
[WARM] they'd want us finishing the run, not falling apart over it
[SUSPICIOUS] i don't trust it either, not since the damage. keep your eyes open
[DRY] it's a machine that thinks it's clever. so am i, and i still break things
[RESIGNED] gotta admit, tin ears made the right call back there
[LAUGHS] don't let it hear you say that, it'll get insufferable
[DEADPAN] i've heard worse jokes from a man with a head wound. barely
[LAUGHS] that's almost funny. i'd laugh harder if i weren't holding this bulkhead shut
[DRY] gallows humor's the only kind that survives long haul runs
[GRUFF] apology accepted. don't let it happen twice, i'm too old to keep count
[WARM] forget it. we've all snapped at somebody by hour twenty of a shift
[PAINED] it's not as bad as it looks. mostly
[PAINED] hurts. won't stop me working, so don't fuss
[GRIM] that's a bad wound. sit down before you fall down, i mean it
[URGENT] hull's breached, i need that seal held while i weld
[TERRIFIED] that's vacuum on the other side, don't you dare open that hatch
[GRIM] locking that door was the only call that saved the rest of us. doesn't make it right
[EXHAUSTED] fixed. patched. rerouted. i've earned a long sit somewhere quiet
[PROUD] that's some of my best work under that kind of pressure, i'll say it once
[WARM] good work yourself. couldn't have held that line without you
[SIGHS] crisis is done. i'll believe it once i've had a full night's sleep
[FLIRTY] careful, Roz, keep talking like that and i'll start believing you mean it
[EMBARRASSED] i, uh. yeah. i was hoping you'd say something like that
[WARM] took you long enough to say it out loud
[RESIGNED] i think the world of you, Roz. i'm just too old and too tired to start something new
[GRUFF] not now. maybe not ever. it's not you, it's every ship i've buried a feeling on
[DRY] captain, with respect, that plan's held together with hope and duct tape
[GRUFF] i'll follow the order. doesn't mean i have to like it
[WARM] you did good today, captain. i don't say that to just anybody
[NERVOUS] power's too low for what you're asking. i can try, no promises
[GRIM] air's thinning. i need everyone out of the corridor while i work
[GRIM] not sure this old girl's got another crisis in her. neither am i, honestly
[GRUFF] combat's not my department. i just keep the lights on while you handle it
[PAINED] took a knock. i'll live. focus on whoever's worse off
[MUTTERS] reminds me of a run on the Kestrel. didn't end well then either
[WARM] i remember my first ship too. thought i knew everything. i knew nothing
[DRY] i've heard bigger boasts from smaller engines
[LAUGHS] you talk a big game for someone who just tripped over a cable
[GRUFF] watch your mouth, i built half this engine room with these hands
[TIRED] all right. i've got work. go bother somebody else for a while
catch you later. try not to break anything before i see you next
[WARM] good talk. get some rest, you look like i feel
[GRUFF] that's enough chatting, this reactor won't fix itself
[DRY] i'll be down here if the ship decides to fall apart again
[RESIGNED] go get some sleep. i mean it this time
[WARM] appreciate you listening to an old man complain
[GRIM] i'll keep you posted if the numbers change. try not to worry in the meantime
[CALM] it's handled. go do whatever you were doing before i interrupted
[EXHAUSTED] i'm done for the day. if it breaks again, it can wait till morning
[GRIM] we'll talk about the door later. not right now, i can't
[WARM] rest easy tonight. we earned it
[SIGHS] i'll raise a mug to them tonight. that's all i know how to do
[FLIRTY] think about what i said, Roz. i'm not in a hurry, but i'm not made of stone either
[EMBARRASSED] anyway. forget i said anything. or don't. your call
[WARM] we're good, captain. go run your ship
[GRUFF] noted. i'll get it done. now let me work
[DRY] i'll believe the quiet shift when it's over, not before
[TIRED] back to it. the ship doesn't fix itself, much as i wish it would
[WARM] thanks for asking. don't get asked that enough down here
[NERVOUS] disease numbers climbing. i don't like being the one who can't fix that kind of broken
[WARM] doc says isolate them. i say trust the doc, she's saved more of us than luck has
power's rationed, food's rationed, patience is rationed. everything's rationed on a long haul
[URGENT] captain, i need that power rerouted from cargo or i can't hold life support
[GRIM] i've patched a hundred hull leaks. this one's got teeth
[PLEADING] just hold the seal a little longer, please, i'm almost there
[SUSPICIOUS] ever notice it answers the question you didn't ask? machines shouldn't guess
[DRY] i'll trust the computer the day it bleeds like the rest of us
[PROUD] not bad for a piece of junk i patched together twice this month
[WARM] you did all right today. proud of you, for what that's worth from an old grease monkey
[GRIM] combat's over. now comes the part where i count the damage
[PAINED] shrapnel in the hull, shrapnel in my arm. matching set
[GRIM] never good at the words for this part. just know they mattered
[WARM] quiet shift's a gift. don't waste it complaining, spend it resting
mess hall smells almost like real food today. almost
[GRUFF] eat something before you fall over. that's not a request
[TIRED] shift's starting and the coffee hasn't kicked in yet. give me a minute
[WARM] good shift, all told. nothing exploded. i'll take it
[GRUFF] i don't do romance talk. i do repairs. but, uh. you're all right, Roz
[FLIRTY] you're the only person on this ship who argues with me and wins
[EMBARRASSED] didn't mean for that to come out. but i meant it
[WARM] you're not so bad yourself, old man. don't let it go to your head
[DRY] captain, i say this with love: your plan needs a plan B
[CONFIDENT] give the word and it's done, captain. i don't miss on the important ones
[GRUFF] i've served under captains who couldn't find the bridge. you're not one of them
[MUTTERS] every captain thinks they're the first to ask me to go faster. none of 'em are
[WARM] Roz keeps this place running as much as i do. more, some weeks
[DEADPAN] i don't do compliments easy. that was me trying
[GRIM] AI's rerouted three systems wrong since the damage. somebody should be watching it closer
[SUSPICIOUS] i'm not saying it's gone rogue. i'm saying keep a hand on the manual override
[GRIM] locked that door myself. i'll hear it clang shut every time i close my eyes tonight
[PLEADING] don't ask me to justify it. i did what kept the rest of you breathing
[WARM] rest now. the ship'll still need fixing tomorrow, i promise you that much
[GRUFF] i don't scare easy. this is one of maybe five times in thirty years
[PANICKED] i don't know how to fix this one, and i always know how to fix it
[TERRIFIED] i'm not ready to lose another one, i'm not, i'm not
[PANICKED] hull's opening faster than i can patch, somebody get me another set of hands
[SHOUTS] clear the corridor, now, i mean it, NOW
[TERRIFIED] can't get a full breath, the air's too thin, i need a mask somebody
[GRUFF] i'll die in this engine room before i let it die first
[PROUD] thirty-one years, four ships, and i've never lost the engine room. not once
[MUTTERS] every one of these panels has a name for me. don't ask which one's my favorite
[WARM] corridor's colder than it should be. somebody tell life support i said hello
[GRUFF] life support's my second home. first one's an engine room, obviously
[DRY] cargo bay's quiet. i almost trust that. almost
[GRIM] i don't like being this close to the airlock without a suit on
[SUSPICIOUS] ai core gives me the creeps lately. don't know why, just does
[GRUFF] bridge's too shiny for my taste. i like grease under my boots, not carpet
[WARM] captain runs a tight bridge. i respect that, even up here
[GRIM] medbay's too full for my liking today
[WARM] quarters could use better insulation. i've said this for years, nobody listens
[GRUFF] Roz, tell me you found what's rattling in bay two
[DRY] you fixed it before i even finished diagnosing it. show-off
[WARM] good work today, Roz. don't let it go to your head, but good work
[GRUFF] you two arguing with the reactor again? it can't hear you, you know
[LAUGHS] she'll tell you the ship's got a mood. she's not wrong, half the time
[GRIM] i keep waiting for the day the doubt's wrong. hasn't come yet
[GRIM] not sure any of us are cut out for another run like this one
[GRUFF] i've got doubts about this plan and a coffee tin full of good bolts. one of those i trust
[WARM] you asked how i'm holding up. nobody usually asks. means something
[GRUFF] offer's there if you need an extra set of hands, captain. don't wait too long to take it
[WARM] i'll cover your shift if you need the sleep. i've got nothing better to do at 3am anyway
[GRUFF] don't thank me. thank the guy who built this engine sixty years ago and made it stubborn
[MUTTERS] every part of this ship's got history i wasn't around for. i respect that
[SIGHS] i miss dirt under my nails that wasn't engine grease
[WARM] first ship i ever worked, i broke more than i fixed. learned fast after that
[GRIM] i remember the last time a hull breach was this bad. didn't end well for two of us
[GRUFF] don't insult my work twice, i heard you the first time
[DRY] that's rich, coming from someone who still can't tell a wrench from a spanner
[RESIGNED] that was out of line. i'm sorry, i was scared and it came out as anger
[GRUFF] i'm not one for sorry, but. sorry. there. said it
[FLIRTY] Roz. come sit with me a minute. i'll even share the good coffee
[EMBARRASSED] i was thinking, when we make port, maybe you and me get off this ship for an evening
[WARM] i'd like to take you to dinner somewhere that isn't the mess hall. that's me asking
[FLIRTY] thirty years of not asking anybody out. figured i'd start with you
[NERVOUS] i rehearsed this and it's still coming out clumsy. Roz, i like you. plainly
morning
hey
evening
[DRY] still here. still standing. still yours to boss around, captain
nothing much to report. bolts are still bolts
engine's purring. don't jinx it by asking twice
all systems green, for once. i'll enjoy it while it lasts
[DRY] pressure's nominal. temperature's nominal. my mood is, as ever, not
[GRUFF] Roz, hand me the 12mil, not the 10, i've told you twice
[DEADPAN] i swapped the filter myself. figured you had enough on your plate
run the numbers again before we commit, captain. i've been burned by hasty math
[GRUFF] give me the manifest, i'll tell you what's actually salvageable versus what sales thinks is
[WARM] you handle the wiring, i'll handle the plumbing. divide and conquer
[DRY] i checked your work. it's good. don't let that go to your head either
[LAUGHS] you should've seen the look on your face when that valve popped
[DRY] you snore. i can hear it through the bulkhead. just so you know
[LAUGHS] captain, you drive this ship like you stole it. i mean that as a compliment. mostly
[DEADPAN] careful captain, keep flying like that and i'll start charging combat pay for my nerves
[LAUGHS] that's the worst idea i've heard all week, and i've heard some real ones
[CONFIDENT] i've got a plan. it involves duct tape and spite. works every time
[CALM] we've survived worse with less. i've got the scars to prove it
[WARM] you're doing better than you think you are. i've watched you work
[GRIM] i can't promise it'll be fine. i can promise i won't stop trying
[PLEADING] just give me another minute, i'm so close, don't pull me off this now
[URGENT] i need Roz down here, this is a two-person job and i'm one person
[GRUFF] somebody get me a fire suppressant canister, panel's sparking
[WARM] tell me what you need and i'll get it done, that's the job
[GRUFF] don't struggle alone with it. i've got two working hands and nothing better to do
[DRY] i'll teach you the trick to that valve if you stop breaking it my way
[PROUD] forty years and i still know every rivet on a ship like this blind
[PROUD] nobody keeps a reactor running smoother than me. that's not bragging, that's a fact
[SUSPICIOUS] i've got half a mind to pull the ai's diagnostic logs myself and see what it's not telling us
[SUSPICIOUS] every time i ask it a straight question i get a straighter answer than i trust
[DRY] fine, the computer called that one right. once
[WARM] i'll say it plain, that computer's saved this crew more than once. i keep score
[PAINED] rib's cracked, i think. don't fuss, i've had worse from a bad ladder
[PAINED] burned my hand on the coupling. price of doing business down here
[GRIM] that wound needs more than a field bandage. go see the doc, i mean it
[URGENT] everybody strap in, we're about to feel that hit
[SHOUTS] incoming, brace!
[TERRIFIED] i don't have a weapon down here, i've got a wrench and bad knees
[PANICKED] everything's on fire, i mean actually on fire, somebody get extinguishers down here
[PANICKED] i can't hold this seal, it's slipping, i need help RIGHT NOW
[PANICKED] too many alarms, i can't tell which one to fix first
[GRIM] good work getting everyone through that. i mean it
[RESIGNED] i keep thinking about the ones we didn't save. figured you would too
[GRIM] i'll fix what i can fix. i can't fix losing someone, never could
[WARM] we keep going. that's what they'd want, and it's what i need too
[MUTTERS] i keep a mental list of everyone i've lost on a ship. wish it stayed shorter
[DRY] disease outbreak's the kind of thing that makes an engine failure look friendly
[GRIM] keep everyone hydrated and separated till the doc clears them. that's the whole plan
[WARM] scrubbers are back online. breathe easy, i mean that literally
[TIRED] rerouted power from cargo heating. we'll be a little cold, we won't be dead
[GRUFF] don't touch the breaker panel, i just got it stable
[WARM] hatch is secure. corridor's safe to cross again
[DRY] i've patched worse holes with less. this one just took more cursing
[WARM] good crew, this one. better than some i've shipped with
[GRUFF] you're all right, for a captain that talks too fast
[DEADPAN] don't wait on me for small talk, i'm elbow deep in a manifold
[WARM] good chat. corridor's colder than the bridge, get moving before you catch something
[TIRED] i'll see you at the next crisis. try not to make it too soon
[WARM] rest up, Roz. i mean it. you've earned it same as me
[GRUFF] go on, get out of my engine room, some of us have work to do
[DRY] i'll be here. i'm always here. it's practically my address now
